nextAction.detail: Per NYC Admin Code § 28-207.2.3, DOB will NOT rescind SWO until all civil penalties are paid — even if inspection passes. Ask Damian ASAP.

timelineDone.desc: Partial Stop Work Order issued. Two violations: electrical (39177537R) and construction (39177526L). 60-day cure filing window starts.
First work permit issued for 6A renovation.
SWO cleared for commercial space (not 6A).
Allan meets with board members. Learns Jack Jaffa is an expeditor (not a law firm). ECB hearings are optional.
Electrical corrective work completed in 6A (BX wiring for lighting, receptacles, switches).
First Certificate of Correction filed for electrical violation (COC0393020).
First request for SWO rescission inspection sent to DOB.
First work permit expires (no work possible under SWO anyway).
SWO rescission follow-up #2 sent to DOB.
Damian update: electrical CP paid, GC civil penalty still waiting on DOB for amount.
SWO rescission follow-up #3 sent to DOB. GC CP request now 13 business days with no response.
$15,000 electrical civil penalty paid via certified check to NYC DOB.
Second COC filed for construction violation (COC0395691) — summons 39177526L.
Allan asks attorney: "Can 6A resume after just 6A SWO rescind, or must ALL SWOs clear?"
First COC approved. Electrical violation 39177537R = RESOLVED in BIS.
Damian: "Great! I will take care of the other one as well."
DOB asks Damian to send permits/applications/fees PDFs to OBM before scheduling rescission inspection.
New work permit issued (#429322759, expires May 31). Cannot use until SWO lifted.
Second COC actually submitted to AEU (by Agnieszka Muczynski, not Damian).
Damian confirms docs sent to OBM for rescission inspection.
Full status check-in email sent to entire team.
Attorney Eric Wohl: "I can't speak to SWO removals. That's an expeditor question."
Allan finds on DOB NOW: GC civil penalty review (670218027) shows APPROVED.
Pawel speaks with Damian. Damian will follow up with DOB. Does NOT answer COC or penalty questions.
Allan calls AEU x3. COC pending. Normal review: 4–5 weeks. Owner can go to 280 Broadway in person. Expediter CANNOT go.
Allan calls OBM. They don't see April 18 deadline — only May 6 hearing. (Confirms April 18 is cure window, not penalty cliff.)
Allan emails OBM supervisor re: SWO rescission requirements.
Brooklyn Construction Enforcement: "We have nothing to do with this." OBM is the only office for SWO rescission.
Lenny Vicari (OBM Supervising Inspector) replies in 14 minutes — offers re-inspection Friday April 10, 8–10 AM.
Allan confirms Friday April 10, 8–10 AM. Pawel on-site (confirmed).

DOB inspector visits — SWO rescinded for 6A.
Can 6A resume after just 6A SWO rescind?

flowchart.text: File Certificate of Correction (COC) with AEU

flowchart.note: Or go in person to 280 Broadway (owner only, deed + ID)

flowchart.text: Submit docs to OBM for SWO rescission

survivalGuide.keyInsight: COC approval and SWO rescission may be INDEPENDENT tracks. You might not need the COC approved before the SWO can be lifted. Confirm with OBM.

contacts.name: AEU (Administrative Enforcement Unit)
contacts.desc: COC review status, civil penalty status

contacts.name: Lenny Vicari — Supervising Inspector (OBM)
contacts.desc: Direct contact for SWO rescission inspection at 176 Johnson St

contacts.name: Brooklyn Construction Enforcement
contacts.desc: ❌ Dead end for SWO — referred us to OBM

tips.title: Your expediter can't do everything.
tips.body: Two out of three AEU reps told me only the owner can go to 280 Broadway in person to speed up a COC review. Bring your deed and ID. Don't assume your expediter is handling it — verify.

tips.title: Call multiple times.
tips.body: I called AEU three times and got different info each time. One rep said my expediter could come. Two said only the owner. Triangulate.

tips.title: COC and SWO may be separate tracks.
tips.body: AEU reviews your COC. OBM handles the SWO. One rep told me they don't think COC approval is needed before the SWO can be lifted. Push both simultaneously.

tips.title: The 60-day deadline IS for filing, not approval — and we met it.
tips.body: Per 1 RCNY §102-01, the 60-day 'cure' window is about filing the COC (done April 3). The $5K+$1K/day penalty we initially feared comes from §28-219.1, which only applies to Class 1 (immediately hazardous) violations — not our Class 2 WWP. Neither AEU nor OBM flagged April 18 as a crisis. May 6 OATH hearing is the real action date.

tips.title: DOB gets frustrated with too many follow-ups.
tips.body: My contractor warned me: expediters can push to a point, then DOB pushes back. Balance persistence with patience.

tips.title: Check DOB NOW yourself.
tips.body: Don't wait for your expediter. Go to the DOB NOW portal and look up your property. I found my civil penalty review was approved before anyone told me.

tips.title: Go to 280 Broadway.
tips.body: If your COC review is taking too long, go in person. 280 Broadway, lower Manhattan, 8:30–3:45 weekdays. Deed + government ID. Highest-impact move an owner can make.

tips.title: Document everything.
tips.body: Every call, text, email. I built this entire site because it's the only way to track what's happening across 10+ people and 3 government agencies.

tips.title: Know AEU vs. OBM vs. ECB.
tips.body: AEU reviews your Certificate of Correction. OBM handles SWO rescission and inspections. ECB handles hearings and penalty adjudication. They're separate.

tips.title: Jack Jaffa is an expeditor, not a law firm.
tips.body: Despite the name, Jack Jaffa & Associates handles DOB paperwork. Legal defense is separate.

openQuestions.q: Has the GC civil penalty actually been PAID?
openQuestions.why: NYC Admin Code § 28-207.2.3 requires all civil penalties paid before SWO rescission. Review is APPROVED but payment is UNKNOWN.
§ 28-207.2.6 — $6K–$12K per violation. Must be paid before rescission. Unknown if any were issued.

openQuestions.q: Does the COC for 39177526L need to be approved before SWO can be rescinded?
openQuestions.why: Evidence suggests no — Lenny scheduled inspection before COC approved. But not explicitly confirmed.

openQuestions.q: Can 6A resume after just 6A SWO rescind?
openQuestions.why: Attorney punted to expediter. If building-wide SWO must clear, timeline extends significantly.
She submitted the COC on April 3, not Damian. Is she at Big Orange? Does this affect anything?

intelLog.intel: "We have nothing to do with this." SWO rescission is exclusively OBM.
intelLog.impact: Eliminates Brooklyn CE as a path. OBM is the only office.

intelLog.intel: Responded to email in 14 minutes. Offered re-inspection Friday April 10. Did NOT mention needing COC approval first.
intelLog.impact: Strongest signal yet that COC and SWO are independent tracks.

intelLog.intel: COC review is 4–5 weeks. Owner can go to 280 Broadway to push. Expediters CANNOT go in person.
intelLog.impact: 280 Broadway trip is owner-only. But may not be needed if SWO can be lifted without COC.

intelLog.intel: GC civil penalty review (670218027) shows APPROVED. But 'approved' may mean review approved, not payment confirmed.
intelLog.impact: Need Damian to confirm actual payment status.

legalRequirements.title: NYC Admin Code Requirements for SWO Rescission

sections.text: All civil penalties must be PAID before SWO rescission.
Work-against-SWO penalties ($6K–$12K each) must also be paid.
Commissioner must inspect and confirm conditions corrected.

displacementCost.note: Should have moved in March 1. SWO made that impossible. Paying $800/week for current apartment we can't leave.
